verses.text: Of the blue, purple, scarlet they made cloths of service for the holy place, and the holy garments for Aaron — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Even the priestly wardrobe is a covenant act. The same fabrics that decorate God's house now clothe the man who enters it. Holiness wraps both the place and the priest.

Made the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen. Beat gold into plates, cut into wires, worked into blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen with cunning work. Shoulderpieces coupled at the edges. The girdle was the same — gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Gold beaten into wire and woven through fabric. The priest's vest is metal and thread inseparable — like the throne behind the vail of gold and cloud. The dress remembers the throne.

The girdle was the same — gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Gold beaten into wire and woven through fabric. The priest's vest is metal and thread inseparable — like the throne behind the vail of gold and cloud. The dress remembers the throne.

Wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold, graven as signets, with the names of the children of Israel. Put them on the shoulders of the ephod — stones for a memorial — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Twelve names on two stones, lifted high on the priest's shoulders every time he goes in to God. The strength of the priest is shaped for the people. Aaron carries them; Christ carries us.

Wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold, graven as signets, with the names of the children of Israel. Put them on the shoulders of the ephod — stones for a memorial — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Twelve names on two stones, lifted high on the priest's shoulders every time he goes in to God. The strength of the priest is shaped for the people. Aaron carries them; Christ carries us.

Made the breastplate like the ephod — gold, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, foursquare, doubled, a span. Four rows of stones, twelve engraved with tribes' names. Wreathen gold chains. Two ouches, two rings, chains joined. Ouches on the shoulderpieces. Two rings inside; two below. Bound to the ephod with blue lace. Guidance: Two stones on the shoulders for strength; twelve on the heart for love. Bound by gold chains and a blue lace. God's people are carried — and kept — in both.

Made the robe of the ephod, woven work, all of blue. A hole in the midst with binding, that it not be rent. On the hem, pomegranates of blue, purple, scarlet, linen. Bells of pure gold between, alternating, to minister — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Bells ring as the priest moves; pomegranates promise the harvest. While the bells sound, Israel knows the priest still lives. While he lives in God's presence, Israel is safe.

Made coats of fine linen, woven, for Aaron and his sons, the mitre of fine linen, goodly bonnets of fine linen, linen breeches of fine twined linen. Girdle of fine twined linen and blue, purple, scarlet, needlework — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Every layer of the priest's clothing is named, every accent. God dresses His servants thoroughly. Holiness is not just the visible robe but the inner garment no one but God sees.

Made coats of fine linen, woven, for Aaron and his sons, the mitre of fine linen, goodly bonnets of fine linen, linen breeches of fine twined linen. Girdle of fine twined linen and blue, purple, scarlet, needlework — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: Every layer of the priest's clothing is named, every accent. God dresses His servants thoroughly. Holiness is not just the visible robe but the inner garment no one but God sees.

God dresses His servants thoroughly. Holiness is not just the visible robe but the inner garment no one but God sees.

Made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold; wrote upon it like the engravings of a signet — HOLINESS TO THE LORD. Tied to it a blue lace to fasten above on the mitre — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The priest's last touch: HOLINESS TO THE LORD shining from his forehead. The mark of the priest is not pedigree, not power, but consecration. Every act of his ministry is read through these four words.

Made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold; wrote upon it like the engravings of a signet — HOLINESS TO THE LORD. Tied to it a blue lace to fasten above on the mitre — as the LORD commanded Moses. Guidance: The priest's last touch: HOLINESS TO THE LORD shining from his forehead. The mark of the priest is not pedigree, not power, but consecration. Every act of his ministry is read through these four words.

All the work of the tabernacle of the congregation was finished. Israel did according to all the LORD commanded Moses. They brought the tabernacle to Moses — the tent and all its furniture, taches, boards, bars, pillars, sockets. Guidance: All the work finished. The same word — finished — that Genesis 2 uses of creation. God's people, after the calf, rebuild a little world for Him. Then they bring it to Moses for inspection.

Rams' skins dyed red, badgers' skins, vail; ark with staves, mercy seat; table, vessels, shewbread; candlestick, lamps, vessels, oil; golden altar, anointing oil, sweet incense, door hanging; brass altar with grate and vessels; laver and foot; court hangings, pillars, sockets, gate hanging, cords, pins; vessels of service; cloths of service; holy garments for Aaron and his sons. Guidance: A second full inventory. The list of chapter 35 returns as completed reality. God's commands end up as named, finished objects on Moses' floor.

According to all the LORD commanded Moses, Israel made all the work. Moses looked upon all the work — they had done it as the LORD commanded. And Moses blessed them. Guidance: Moses looked, and like God on creation's seventh day, he saw it was good. Then he blessed them. The chapter ends with the same shape as Genesis 1–2: finished work, blessing. A new creation in linen.
